machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism (X. euvesicatoria). XpsE is the ATPase: 'deletion of genes encoding the putative ATPase XpsE or the secretin XpsD leads to reduced virulence'. ATPase function also characterized in X. campestris pv. campestris Xps system (Shiue 2006, PMID 16525507).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism. GspF-family central IM platform component ('XpsC, XpsL and XpsM ... likely surround the central XpsF component of the assembly platform').

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism; Goll 2025 lists 'the pseudopilus proteins XpsG, H, I, J and K'. Major-pseudopilin role established in X. campestris pv. campestris Xps system: 'The major pseudopilin XpsG is an essential component of type II secretion apparatus of Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris' (Lee 2004, PMID 15590656).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism; named among 'the pseudopilus proteins XpsG, H, I, J and K'. GspH-family minor pseudopilin from apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism; named among 'the pseudopilus proteins XpsG, H, I, J and K'. GspI-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism; named among 'the pseudopilus proteins XpsG, H, I, J and K'. GspJ-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism; named among 'the pseudopilus proteins XpsG, H, I, J and K'. GspK-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: the predicted assembly platform components XpsC, XpsL and XpsM form a trimeric complex which is essential for T2S and associates with the cytoplasmic ATPase XpsE and the secretin XpsD
machinery_genes.note: Same organism. XpsL is part of the XpsCLM assembly-platform complex and recruits the ATPase XpsE to the membrane (Shiue 2006, PMID 16525507, X. campestris).

machinery_genes.quote: the predicted assembly platform components XpsC, XpsL and XpsM form a trimeric complex which is essential for T2S and associates with the cytoplasmic ATPase XpsE and the secretin XpsD
machinery_genes.note: Same organism. XpsM is the GspM-family member of the trimeric XpsCLM assembly platform.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (links assembly platform to secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: the predicted assembly platform components XpsC, XpsL and XpsM form a trimeric complex which is essential for T2S and associates with the cytoplasmic ATPase XpsE and the secretin XpsD
machinery_genes.note: Same organism. 'XpsC (formerly designated XpsN) was considered as a functional equivalent of GspN ... due to the presence of a predicted HR region and structural similarities with other known GspC proteins, it is hereafter referred to as XpsC.' GspC-family IM platform / secretin link. (Earlier Xcc literature calls it XpsN.)

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the Xps-T2S system from are encoded by a chromosomal cluster of eleven genes and include XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D
machinery_genes.note: Same organism. XpsD is the OM secretin: 'deletion of genes encoding the putative ATPase XpsE or the secretin XpsD leads to reduced virulence'.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures the minor pseudopilins; shared with type IV pilus, encoded outside the cluster)
machinery_genes.quote: A homolog of the prepilin peptidase GspO, which processes the minor pseudopilins prior to pseudopilus assembly, is encoded outside of the -T2S gene cluster
machinery_genes.note: Goll 2025 documents the GspO prepilin peptidase for the X. euvesicatoria Xps system but does not give it an Xps name. The corresponding gene in the X. campestris pv. campestris Xps system is named XpsO (Lee 2004, PMID 15590656: cross-linking 'detectable in a strain missing all components, but XpsO, of the type II secretion apparatus'). Listed because it is functionally part of the Xps apparatus though encoded outside the xpsE-D cluster; lowest-confidence call (the Xps-specific name comes from the Xcc system, not the X. euvesicatoria paper).

notes: The Xps-T2S system is the outer-membrane type II secretion system of Xanthomonas euvesicatoria (causal agent of bacterial spot in pepper/tomato), one of two T2S systems in this organism; it secretes degradative enzymes (xylanases, proteases). The apparatus paper Goll et al 2025 (PLoS Pathog, PMID 40202974) is on this exact organism and explicitly names the eleven-gene chromosomal cluster: XpsE, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, C and D, mapping each to its Gsp family. All 11 cluster genes are therefore documented with no strain transfer. Subcomplex assignment: assembly platform = XpsC/XpsF/XpsL/XpsM + ATPase XpsE; pseudopilus = major XpsG + minor XpsH/I/J/K; secretin = XpsD. The prepilin peptidase GspO is encoded outside the cluster; Goll 2025 documents it for X. euvesicatoria but only the X. campestris pv. campestris Xps literature (Lee 2004, PMID 15590656) gives the Xps-specific name XpsO; included as the 12th, lowest-confidence machinery gene. Supporting biochemical papers from the Hu lab (Shiue 2006 EMBO J ATPase/XpsL interaction; Chen 2005 JBC XpsE N-domain; Lee 2004 JBC XpsG-XpsN-XpsD cross-linking) characterize individual Xps components in X. campestris pv. campestris, the same Xps system family. Deliberately excluded: pilotin GspS and accessory GspA/GspB (Goll 2025 states these are absent in X. euvesicatoria); the Sec/Tat translocon (general IM transport, not T2SS); the secreted substrates/effectors (xylanases, proteases, and the curated effectors XCV0536, XCV0965, XCV3671, XCV4358, XCV4360); and regulators (Clp/cAMP-receptor regulation of xpsE, Ge 2008 PMID 18175178; RpfF/DSF, PMID 34704368).
